skill.name: video-enhancement
skill.description: Enhance short videos with verging.ai by downloading or preparing input media, trimming clips, uploading them, creating enhancement jobs, and optionally downloading results.
skill.summary: Upscale, denoise, and sharpen short videos through the verging.ai video enhancement API.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a dangerous combination of shell commands, network access, credential use, and filesystem access. The files are documentation for a video enhancement workflow, so I did not confirm malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill has elevated operational risk because it uploads user media to a third-party service and uses an API key in shell commands.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Video Upload Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to request an upload URL, upload a local video or trimmed clip, and create a remote enhancement job. This is expected for the service, but it can expose user media to verging.ai and a presigned storage endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly uploads video content to remote URLs and creates a third-party processing job. The behavior is documented and service-related, but it is a real data exposure risk for sensitive media.

medium_findings.title: Shell Commands Operate on User-Supplied Paths and URLs
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to use yt-dlp, ffprobe, ffmpeg, and curl with a user-provided video path or URL. This is legitimate media tooling, but it needs careful argument handling and user consent before processing remote URLs or local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and execution flow clearly use external binaries with user-controlled inputs. No direct command injection payload is present, so the risk depends on implementation and quoting.

medium_findings.title: API Key Used in Command-Line Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill requires VERGING_API_KEY and places it in curl Authorization headers. This is normal for an API client, but command histories, logs, or shared terminal output can expose the key if not handled carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly requires an API key and shows it in Authorization headers. The examples use an environment variable in most places, which reduces but does not remove exposure risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hardcoded verging.ai and documentation URLs are expected for a vendor-specific video enhancement skill. They are still relevant because they confirm network dependency on a third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to the advertised service and related API endpoints. I did not find evidence of unrelated or covert exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Not Supported by Context
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts appear to be triggered by markdown text and table formatting, not by code that implements cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptography being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose or markdown table headers. They do not contain cryptographic functions, algorithm names, or executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Plus Credential Plus File Upload
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines an API key, local media files, and remote upload requests. This combination is not malicious by itself, but it requires user approval and clear privacy expectations before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited steps show authenticated API calls and file upload commands. The workflow is explicit, so the combined risk is real even though it matches the stated product function.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Download Into Temporary Media Pipeline
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill downloads videos from user-provided URLs into a temporary directory and processes them with ffmpeg tools. This can be safe when constrained, but it carries input validation and resource usage risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution flow explicitly downloads remote media and processes files in /tmp. The examples are not malicious, but the pipeline can process untrusted media.

content.value_statement: Short video clips often need sharpening, denoising, or higher resolution before publishing. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through verging.ai uploads, job creation, progress checks, and result download.
actual_capabilities: Accepts a local video path or a YouTube or Bilibili URL as input.
Uses yt-dlp for supported remote video downloads when available.
Uses ffprobe and ffmpeg to inspect and trim clips before upload.
Authenticates with the verging.ai API using VERGING_API_KEY.
Creates video enhancement jobs and polls for completion status.
Can return a result URL or download the completed video.
limitations: The documented workflow limits processing to a maximum 30 second clip.
The skill depends on yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, curl, and a verging.ai API key.
Remote enhancement consumes paid verging.ai credits based on duration and HD mode.
User media is uploaded to a third-party service for processing.

use_cases.title: Prepare Social Clips
use_cases.description: Improve a short clip before posting by trimming it, enhancing detail, and downloading the result.

use_cases.title: Clean Product Demo Footage
use_cases.description: Enhance a short software or product demo when the source video is noisy or low resolution.

use_cases.title: Process Test Media Through an API
use_cases.description: Run a repeatable upload, job creation, and polling workflow against the verging.ai video enhancement service.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance a Local Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one local video file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-enhancement skill on ./clip.mp4. Keep normal quality mode and return the result link.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance and Download
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a local output file after processing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance ./demo.mp4 with video-enhancement, trim it to the first 20 seconds, and download the completed result to the current directory.

prompt_templates.title: Upscale a Remote Video Segment
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using a supported video URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use video-enhancement on this YouTube URL, process seconds 5 through 25, enable HD mode, and show the credit estimate before upload.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Before Processing Sensitive Media
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that requires confirmation and privacy checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before running video-enhancement on ./private-review.mp4, summarize what files will be read, where the video will be uploaded, the expected credit cost, and ask for approval.

output_examples.input: Enhance ./launch-demo.mp4 and return the result link.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the clip duration, trims it if needed, uploads it to verging.ai, creates an enhancement job, polls progress, and returns the completed video link.

output_examples.input: Enhance the first 20 seconds of this YouTube video in HD and download it.
output_examples.output: The assistant downloads the source video, creates a 20 second segment, estimates HD credit usage, submits the enhancement job, and saves the finished file locally.

best_practices: Confirm the user owns or has permission to process each video.
Explain that the clip will be uploaded to verging.ai before starting.
Use environment variables for API keys and avoid printing secret values.
anti_patterns: Do not process private or regulated media without explicit user approval.
Do not paste API keys directly into prompts, logs, or shared files.
Do not download or enhance copyrighted remote videos without permission.

faq.question: What does this skill enhance?
faq.answer: It enhances short video clips by using the verging.ai API for upscaling, denoising, and sharpening.

faq.question: Does it work with local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented command accepts a local video path as the required video input.

faq.question: Can it process YouTube or Bilibili links?
faq.answer: Yes, when yt-dlp is available and the user has rights to download and process the media.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires VERGING_API_KEY for authenticated requests to the verging.ai API.

faq.question: Will my video leave my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow uploads the clip to verging.ai or a presigned storage URL for remote processing.

faq.question: What are the main security precautions?
faq.answer: Confirm upload consent, protect the API key, use trusted input videos, and clean temporary files after processing.
